results.title: Livestock Prices Fall Across Serbia
results.description: Livestock prices declined across much of Serbia last week, with lower prices recorded for calves, fattening bulls, and pigs in several regions. While prices generally trended downward, some local markets reported regional fluctuations, according to STIPS.

results.title: New Lotus high-performance centre opened by industry minister
results.description: Lotus has opened a new high-performance centre, opening its Hethel headquarters to other companies to test and manufacture at the site.

results.title: How to Plan and File Taxes in One Place
results.description: Taxes are an unavoidable part of financial life, yet they are often treated as a last-minute task. Some individuals begin filing without proper planning, while others plan deductions but struggle when it is time to submit their return. This disconnect can lead to confusion, missed benefits, and unnecessary stress. A more structured approach is to plan and file taxes within a single, connected journey. When tax planning and file income tax return are integrated into one streamlined process, the experience becomes clearer, more organised, and more efficient. Why Tax Planning Should Come Before Filing Tax planning lays the groundwork for accurate filing. It helps you estimate your annual income, understand your potential tax liability, evaluate eligible deductions, and compare tax regimes before the filing deadline approaches. Without planning, filing may feel rushed and reactive. You may realise too late that certain deductions could have reduced your tax outgo or that an alternative tax regime may have been more suitable. Planning in advance ensures that filing your income tax return becomes a structured continuation of decisions already made. Understanding Your Income and Deductions Effective tax planning begins with clarity around your income sources. These may include: Salary income Business or freelance income Interest from savings accounts or fixed deposits Capital gains or other taxable income Once income is identified, the next step is to review applicable deductions under the applicable tax regime. Structured planning tools help present these deductions in a simplified format, making it easier to understand their impact on overall tax liability. This preparation ensures that, when you file an income tax return , key details are already accounted for. Comparing Tax Regimes with Clarity Choosing between the old and new tax regimes is one of the most important decisions in today’s tax environment. Each regime offers a different structure of slab rates and deductions. A comparison based on your actual income and eligible deductions allows you to make a data-backed choice instead of relying on assumptions. Selecting the appropriate regime during the planning stage simplifies the filing process later. Filing Without Starting from Scratch Traditionally, planning and filing were treated as separate activities. This often resulted in duplicate work and inconsistent data. When both stages exist within a unified system, information entered during planning flows directly into the filing stage. Income details, deduction selections, and regime choice remain consistent, reducing the risk of manual errors and saving time. Guided Steps for Structured Filing A guided filing experience helps taxpayers complete each section methodically. Step-by-step prompts explain required information and highlight missing inputs where necessary. A structured flow typically includes: Entering income details Declaring deductions and exemptions Reviewing tax calculations Verifying return details before submission Built-in checks help minimise common reporting errors, supporting a smoother filing experience. Flexibility in Filing Approach Taxpayers have different comfort levels with filing. Some prefer a self-guided approach, while others may seek assistance. An integrated digital platform should provide flexibility, allowing users to start independently and access support as needed. This ensures that the process remains accessible without limiting individual choice. Tracking and Confidence After Filing Once your return is filed, having visibility into its status is just as important. A single platform lets you track confirmations, submission status, and updates without confusion. This transparency provides peace of mind and eliminates the need for repeated follow-ups. Over time, this integrated approach also helps you build better tax habits. Each year becomes easier than the last because your financial data and understanding improve with every cycle. One Place For Smarter Tax Decisions Planning and filing taxes together changes how you approach your finances. Instead of reacting to tax deadlines, you start making informed decisions throughout the year. You understand how your income choices affect your taxes, how deductions can be optimised, and how to file income tax return smoothly when the time comes. Platforms like the Jio Finance app bring tax planning and filing together in one seamless experience, allowing you to plan ahead, make informed choices, and file your income tax return without unnecessary complexity. Conclusion Managing taxes does not need to be fragmented or stressful. When planning and filing are integrated into a single process, each step builds on the previous one, from understanding income and deductions to selecting the appropriate tax regime and completing the return. An integrated approach reduces duplication, improves accuracy, and supports timely compliance. Over time, this structured method transforms tax filing from a last-minute obligation into a more organised and confident financial routine. Citation Heading
